documentation.summary:
AlloyDB for PostgreSQL is an open source-compatible database service that provides a powerful option for migrating, modernizing, or building commercial-grade applications. It offers full compatibility with standard PostgreSQL, and is more than 4x faster for transactional workloads and up to 100x faster for analytical queries than standard PostgreSQL in our performance tests. AlloyDB for PostgreSQL offers a 99.99 percent availability SLA inclusive of maintenance.

AlloyDB is optimized for the most demanding use cases, allowing you to build new applications that require high transaction throughput, large database sizes, or multiple read resources; scale existing PostgreSQL workloads with no application changes; and modernize legacy proprietary databases.

monitoredResources.displayName: Internal monitored resource representing a physical node in AlloyDB instance.
monitoredResources.description: Internal monitored resource representing a physical node in AlloyDB instance.

labels.description: The identifier of the GCP project associated with this resource.
The Google Cloud region in which the AlloyDB instance is running.
AlloyDB cluster identifier.
AlloyDB instance identifier.
AlloyDB instance type (READ or PRIMARY).
AlloyDB physical node identifier within the AlloyDB instance.
Service name.

monitoredResources.displayName: Instance identified by UUID. If the node_id metric label is present, then the metric refers to a specific VM of the instance.
monitoredResources.description: Dual-use schema for both instance and VM-level metrics. Adding node_id as a metric field (rather than a target field) guarantees that all metric streams relevant to the same instance will be on the same Monarch leaf node.
